prompt: Explain the attention mechanism in transformer models to a software engineer who has never done ML. Keep it to about 200 words.
response:
Think of a transformer model like a meeting where everyone is talking while trying to answer a specific question. The attention mechanism is how each person decides who to listen to at any moment.

When a word is processed, attention weighs the importance of all other words in context.Imagine the word "saw" - attention knows it means "cutting tool" when near "woodcutter," but "verbing" when near "dental appointment." It calculates numerical "weights" for each relationship and focuses only on relevant information.

The transformer uses these weights to calculate a weighted average of all information, giving prominence to the most relevant words. As you process each word, attention dynamically recalculates these relationships at every step, building a context-aware representation of the input.

This differs from traditional approaches like RNNs/GRUs, which process sequentially and forget earlier information, attention allows parallel processing of entire inputs while preserving rich context relationships. The "self-attention" in transformers is what makes them powerful - each position can directly influence every other position simultaneously with learned importance weights.
